title: Podman doesn't play well with terminal sizing
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)*
/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
I am running two containers in a pod, rootless, and doing django dev in one whilst the other is a postgres image.  I have to exit and re-enter the container quite frequently, as the output on the terminal becomes garbled, and sometimes disappears.  It is more pronounced when using terminology and less so with gnome-terminal.   In both cases I am running Fedora Silverblue as the os, but the issue was present on Fedora workstation before I reinstalled with silverblue.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. enter a container

2. run the django dev server and/or shell.  After four or five minutes or an unknown and possibly random amount of commands, the command line # disappears, and then using the command line history becomes difficult with at best a garbled prompt, which I can clear temporarily by using clear.   Usually I reset the issue by exiting and re-entering the container. 

3.  ---

**Describe the results you received:**
garbled or disappeared text on command prompt

**Describe the results you expected:**
normal terminal usage

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Tue Dec  8 14:37:50 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
podman info --debug
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.18.0
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.21-3.fc33.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.21, commit: 0f53fb68333bdead5fe4dc5175703e22cf9882ab'
  cpus: 2
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "33"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1001
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 165536
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1001
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 165536
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.10.11-200.fc33.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 3975086080
  memTotal: 8215330816
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.17-1.fc33.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.17
      commit: 0e9229ae34caaebcb86f1fde18de3acaf18c6d9a
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.8-1.fc33.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.8
      commit: d361001f495417b880f20329121e3aa431a8f90f
      libslirp: 4.3.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.0
  swapFree: 4107268096
  swapTotal: 4107268096
  uptime: 1h 35m 52.52s (Approximately 0.04 days)
registries:
  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /var/home/dev/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 4
    paused: 0
    running: 3
    stopped: 1
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: fuse-overlayfs-1.4.0-1.fc33.x86_64
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.9.3
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.4
        FUSE library version 3.9.3
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /var/home/dev/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: btrfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 29
  runRoot: /run/user/1001/containers
  volumePath: /var/home/dev/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 2.1.0
  Built: 1607438270
  BuiltTime: Tue Dec  8 14:37:50 2020
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.15.5
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.2.1
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.2.1-1.fc33.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Not sure.  Silverblue's unwriteable directory structure makes it difficult to test different versions, I think, and I have read the issue tracker FAQs and it is not listed there.  It would be helpful if you could place the url of the 'Podman Troubleshooting Guide' in this template.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
my ostree is standard with the exception of terminology, firewall-config, and google-chrome, but it was happening on Workstation before I reinstalled.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @millerthegorilla!

Can you elaborate on the "terminal sizing"? Is the Django terminal resized before the symptoms occur?

No, the terminal is sized as normal, in the case of gnome terminal (I haven't used terminology for a while).
I don't resize it, the issue just happens normally.

When using bpython as a shell for django, inside the container, I get the following error, which I think might be related:
```
 File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsiesfrontend/replpainter.py", line 33, in display_linize
    display_lines = list(msg.width_aware_splitlines(columns))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/curtsies/formatstring.py", line 654, in width_aware_splitlines
    raise ValueError("Column width %s is too narrow." % columns)
ValueError: Column width 0 is too narrow.

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/django_forum/django_forum/./manage.py", line 22, in <module>
    main()
  File "/opt/django_forum/django_forum/./manage.py", line 18, in main
    execute_from_command_line(sys.argv)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/__init__.py", line 401, in execute_from_command_line
    utility.execute()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/__init__.py", line 395, in execute
    self.fetch_command(subcommand).run_from_argv(self.argv)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/base.py", line 330, in run_from_argv
    self.execute(*args, **cmd_options)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/base.py", line 371, in execute
    output = self.handle(*args, **options)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/commands/shell.py", line 100, in handle
    return getattr(self, shell)(options)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/commands/shell.py", line 40, in bpython
    bpython.embed()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/__init__.py", line 42, in embed
    return main(args, locals_, banner)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsies.py", line 221, in main
    exit_value = repl.mainloop(True, paste)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsies.py", line 125, in mainloop
    self.process_event_and_paint(None)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsies.py", line 107, in process_event_and_paint
    array, cursor_pos = self.paint()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsiesfrontend/repl.py", line 1515, in paint
    current_line = paint.paint_current_line(
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsiesfrontend/replpainter.py", line 57, in paint_current_line
    lines = display_linize(current_display_line, columns, True)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsiesfrontend/replpainter.py", line 39, in display_linize
    range(0, len(msg), columns),
ValueError: range() arg 3 must not be zero
root@pod_forum:/opt/django_forum/django_forum# ./manage.py shell
/static/ /static/

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsiesfrontend/replpainter.py", line 33, in display_linize
    display_lines = list(msg.width_aware_splitlines(columns))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/curtsies/formatstring.py", line 654, in width_aware_splitlines
    raise ValueError("Column width %s is too narrow." % columns)
ValueError: Column width 0 is too narrow.

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/django_forum/django_forum/./manage.py", line 22, in <module>
    main()
  File "/opt/django_forum/django_forum/./manage.py", line 18, in main
    execute_from_command_line(sys.argv)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/__init__.py", line 401, in execute_from_command_line
    utility.execute()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/__init__.py", line 395, in execute
    self.fetch_command(subcommand).run_from_argv(self.argv)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/base.py", line 330, in run_from_argv
    self.execute(*args, **cmd_options)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/base.py", line 371, in execute
    output = self.handle(*args, **options)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/commands/shell.py", line 100, in handle
    return getattr(self, shell)(options)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/django/core/management/commands/shell.py", line 40, in bpython
    bpython.embed()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/__init__.py", line 42, in embed
    return main(args, locals_, banner)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsies.py", line 221, in main
    exit_value = repl.mainloop(True, paste)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsies.py", line 125, in mainloop
    self.process_event_and_paint(None)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsies.py", line 107, in process_event_and_paint
    array, cursor_pos = self.paint()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsiesfrontend/repl.py", line 1515, in paint
    current_line = paint.paint_current_line(
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsiesfrontend/replpainter.py", line 57, in paint_current_line
    lines = display_linize(current_display_line, columns, True)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/bpython/curtsiesfrontend/replpainter.py", line 39, in display_linize
    range(0, len(msg), columns),
ValueError: range() arg 3 must not be zero
```
It might be something completely different, but I thought I'd mention it since it seems to have issues with painting to the terminal.
Is it possible that the value for columns should be set but itsn't?
I'm afraid I don't know a great deal about the terminal setup and how podman containers might interact with it.
Are there any useful commands I can run to report what the shell can see of the terminal itself?
COLUMNS seems to come from bash, not available via sh
Can you try forcing a resize by sending Podman a SIGWINCH?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
there was no feedback since February.  Please reopen if you've more data
